events.description: The new `previousVersion` documentation here refers to the "previous certificate", but the same feat

details.body: The new `previousVersion` documentation here refers to the "previous certificate", but the same feature is described in this PR's CHANGELOG as the "previous secret". Please verify the intended meaning from the service spec and make the wording consistent across docs (model field + getter + CHANGELOG), e.g., clarify whether this applies only to certificate-backed secrets or all secrets.

events.description: `tsp-location.yaml` diverges from the other Key Vault modules: it drops `cleanup: true` and the `add

details.body: `tsp-location.yaml` diverges from the other Key Vault modules: it drops `cleanup: true` and the `additionalDirectories` entry no longer ends with a trailing `/`. Other Key Vault libraries keep `cleanup: true` and use trailing slashes for additionalDirectories paths (for example `azure-security-keyvault-keys/tsp-location.yaml`). Consider restoring `cleanup: true` and using `specification/keyvault/data-plane/Secrets/common/` for consistency and to avoid tooling differences when resolving directory

## Pull request overview

Updates `azure-security-keyvault-secrets` based on TypeSpec SDK generation for Key Vault Secrets (API version `2025-07-01`), aligning repo configuration and generated models/client surfaces with the latest spec.

**Changes:**
- Update TypeSpec source location (`tsp-location.yaml`) to the new `specification/keyvault/data-plane/Secrets` directory and spec commit.
- Add `previousVersion` to generated secret response models and reflect it in the generated implementation cli

events.description: The top CHANGELOG entry was changed from "(Unreleased)" to a specific date. In this repo, Key Vault

details.body: The top CHANGELOG entry was changed from "(Unreleased)" to a specific date. In this repo, Key Vault packages keep the next version marked as "Unreleased" until the release is finalized (see `azure-security-keyvault-keys/CHANGELOG.md`). Also, this section removed the standard empty subsections (Breaking Changes/Other Changes). Consider reverting to the standard format and keeping the entry as Unreleased.

`additionalDirectories` is currently parsed as null because the list item on the next line isn’t ind

details.body: `additionalDirectories` is currently parsed as null because the list item on the next line isn’t indented under the key. This makes the YAML structure incorrect for tools that read `tsp-location.yaml`. Indent the list item(s) under `additionalDirectories:` (and consider removing the trailing space after the colon).

events.description: The 1.5.0 entry no longer mentions the API/service version bump to `2025-07-01` that this generation

details.body: The 1.5.0 entry no longer mentions the API/service version bump to `2025-07-01` that this generation PR is based on, even though prior entries consistently call out service version upgrades in the changelog. Please add an `Other Changes` (or similar) bullet noting the service/API version upgrade so the release notes remain complete and consistent.

**Changes:**
- Updates TypeSpec source location metadata and module assets tag for the new generation input.
- Reworks time serialization to use `azcore/runtime/datetime` types (removing the local `time_unix.go` helper).

`metadata.json` declares API version `2025-07-01` and cross-language IDs for types like `KnownConten

details.body: `metadata.json` declares API version `2025-07-01` and cross-language IDs for types like `KnownContentType`, but the published sources under `src/` (and the generated API review files added in this PR) don’t expose those types or the `2025-07-01` version (e.g., `src/models` still only lists 7.5/7.6). This looks like an incomplete regeneration/sync where `generated/` was updated but `src/` wasn’t. Please either regenerate/update the `src/api` + `src/models` surfaces to match `2025-07-01`, or adjus

## Pull request overview

This PR appears to be an automated SDK generation update for `@azure/keyvault-secrets`, targeting the Key Vault Secrets TypeSpec configuration and declaring API version `2025-07-01`. It also expands packaging/build outputs to support additional entrypoints and platforms.

**Changes:**
- Updates build/export configuration to add `./api` and `./models` entrypoints and React Native build target.
- Updates TypeSpec location metadata and adds a `metadata.json` with API versi

`pnpm-lock.yaml` changed `@azure/monitor-opentelemetry-exporter` from a workspace `link:` dependency to a registry-resolved `1.0.0-beta.39` for at least one importer. Since this repo contains a workspace package at `sdk/monitor/monitor-opentelemetry-exporter`, this can cause pnpm to pull the registry package instead of using the local workspace version (breaking monorepo consistency). Please regenerate the lockfile using the repo’s standard workspace-linking settings so workspace packages remain

`//metadata.constantPaths` now points at `generated/api/keyVaultContext.ts` (`userAgentInfo`), but this package’s main entrypoint (`SecretClient`) uses `src/constants.ts` (`SDK_VERSION`) to build its user agent. With the current change, version bump automation may stop updating `SDK_VERSION`, leading to incorrect telemetry/user-agent version reporting for the primary client. Consider restoring `constantPaths` to `src/constants.ts` (prefix `SDK_VERSION`), or include both constants if you intend t

details.body: The package has real `samples-dev/*` and a `tsconfig.samples.json`, but `build:samples`, `execute:samples`, and `generate:client` were changed to `echo skipped`, and the `//sampleConfiguration` block was removed. This is likely to break sample build/publish automation and reduces the ability to validate samples as part of CI. Please restore the previous sample-related scripts and `//sampleConfiguration` (or an equivalent) unless the repo has explicitly migrated this package away from sample vali

events.description: 2 tool issues found in this PR (`@azure/keyvault-secrets` v4.10.2, targeting API version `2025-07-01

details.body:
2 tool issues found in this PR (`@azure/keyvault-secrets` v4.10.2, targeting API version `2025-07-01`). CI checks are all passing. No public API naming, type-safety, or export violations were found beyond the issues below.

<details>
<summary>📊 Structured Report</summary>

```json
{"agent":"mgmt-reviewer","pr":37724,"summary":"issues_found","findings":[{"file":"sdk/keyvault/keyvault-secrets/generated/api/keyVaultContext.ts","line":31,"issueType":"tool","category":"version-alignment","descriptio

🔴 **Tool Issue** — `generated/api/keyVaultContext.ts:31`

```ts
const userAgentInfo = `azsdk-js-keyvault-secrets/1.0.0-beta.1`;
```

The hardcoded user-agent version `1.0.0-beta.1` does not match the current package version `4.10.2` in `package.json`. This means telemetry will report the wrong SDK version. Additionally, the `//metadata.constantPaths` in `package.json` only references `src/constants.ts` (for `SDK_VERSION`), so future version bumps will not auto-update this generated context file

🔴 **Tool Issue** — `review/keyvault-secrets-models-node.api.md:65-69`

```ts
export enum KnownVersions {
    V75 = "7.5",
    V76 = "7.6",
    V76Preview2 = "7.6-preview.2"
}
```

This PR targets API version `2025-07-01` (per `metadata.json` and the PR description), and the auto-generated `generated/models/models.ts` correctly includes `V20250701 = "2025-07-01"` in `KnownVersions`. However, `src/models/models.ts` — which backs the public API report — does **not** include `V20250701`, so users c

## Pull request overview

**Changes:**

events.description: README describes this package as a generic “Azure KeyVault client” and says it performs “cryptograph

details.body: README describes this package as a generic “Azure KeyVault client” and says it performs “cryptographic key operations”, but this package is `@azure/keyvault-certificates` and the primary public surface is CertificateClient. This description is misleading for users and should be updated to specifically describe certificate management and reference CertificateClient (and related certificate operations) as the main entry point.
